summary: An oil-rich North African state fractured by civil war since the 2011 fall of Gaddafi, split between rival governments in Tripoli and the east and a magnet for foreign powers and migration routes.

history.detail: Libya becomes a kingdom under Idris I after Italian colonial rule and WWII occupation.
A young officer overthrows the monarchy in a bloodless coup.
Reagan bombs Libya after the Berlin disco attack.
Libya gives up nuclear and chemical weapons; sanctions lifted.
Arab Spring uprising; NATO airstrikes; Gaddafi captured and killed.
Country splits between the Tripoli government and Haftar's eastern forces.

key_doctrines: Control of oil terminals as leverage

finance.currency_role: Libyan dinar (LYD); rival central banks have undermined it
finance.central_bank: Central Bank of Libya (contested between factions)

resources.oil_gas: Largest proven oil reserves in Africa; production disrupted by conflict
resources.water: Severely scarce; the Great Man-Made River taps fossil aquifers
resources.agriculture: Limited to the coastal strip; heavily import-dependent

follow_the_money.detail: Rival governments fight over National Oil Corporation income
Tens of billions of LIA funds frozen abroad since 2011
Smuggling networks profit from Europe-bound migration through Libya

theories_disputed.detail: Libya accepted responsibility and paid compensation for the 1988 Pan Am 103 bombing.
Russian Wagner and Syrian/Turkish-backed fighters confirmed on both sides.

connections.detail: Backed the Tripoli government with drones and troops
Wagner mercenaries supported Haftar's eastern forces
Former colonial ruler; key energy and migration partner
